TextENGNew: Rena had lost the match, but she'd claimed Rika-chan — and Satoko as a bonus — as her "reward."
Right hand stroking Rika-chan's head. Left hand stroking Satoko's head. A girl on each arm, she kept chanting "So kyute? ☆" as she drifted home on a cloud of pure bliss.

She really did look happy, I'll give her that. They say sometimes losing is winning — and maybe she had a point...
But what about me, abandoned and alone in front of the shoe lockers?
changeReason: Old: 'Lena's face really looked happy'; New: 'She really did look happy'. Old uses 'Lena' which is incorrect — the character's name is Rena (レナ). However the more significant change is old drops the second half: 'I was left alone in front of the shoe box' vs new: 'But what about me, abandoned and alone in front of the shoe lockers?' — old lacks the self-deprecating questioning tone of the Japanese.

TextENGNew: A sharp smack landed on my back. Mion.
changeReason: Old: 'It was the sound of Mion'; New: 'A sharp smack landed on my back. Mion.' Japanese 背中をパシンと叩かれた。魅音だ describes being slapped on the back and identifying Mion — old says 'sound of Mion' which is awkward and inaccurate.

TextENGNew:
"Lucky you, Kei-chan.
Now you get to walk home alone with me, just the two of us."
"I won, didn't I? This feels an awful lot like a penalty game. Hau~"

"You're terrible!
Ahahahaha!"
My back still throbbed. Mion laughed it off as a "whip of love," breezy as ever — but there was no getting around it. This was a penalty game, plain and simple.
changeReason: Old: 'I'm not sure if this is a punishment game or something else'; New: 'Mion laughed it off as a whip of love, breezy as ever — but there was no getting around it. This was a penalty game, plain and simple.' Old omits the 愛のムチ (whip of love) joke and Mion's carefree laugh, and reverses the certainty — Japanese says it definitely IS a penalty game.

TextENGNew: Rubbing my sore back, I set off for home alongside Mion.

"That was impressive, I'll admit.
Canceling out Rena's Kyute Mode.
I never would have expected you to come at her with a double-offering strategy — Rika-chan and Satoko both at once.
Even I was caught off guard."

"I was just giving it everything I had to win.
But... thinking back on it now, it maybe wasn't the most manly play."

"What are you talking about!
Have you already forgotten the club's cardinal rule?"

"Aim for first place, and first place only.
Do whatever it takes to get there."

"Exactly.
A win is a win.
Hold your head high.
Hey-o — the great Maebara-meijin!
Ahahahaha!"
Swept up by Mion's hearty laughter, I found myself laughing too, before I'd even noticed.
"Well now, we meet again, Keiichi-kun."
Tomitake-san.
I was getting the feeling I ran into him more often than could be explained by chance.
"Hey there. Getting any good shots?"

"Oh, well enough.
So..."
Without warning, Tomitake-san pulled me close by the shoulder and dropped his voice to a murmur.

"My, my, Keiichi-kun. You're quite the smooth operator, aren't you.
A different girl from last time, is it?!"
"It's n-not like that!"

"No need to play dumb!
Experience is everything when you're young, you know～!"
Tomitake-san's crude chuckling gave the whole thing away to Mion even though she couldn't hear a word of the actual whispering.
"Rumor has it you're not exactly standing still yourself, Tomitake-ojisama~?"
changeReason: Old: 'Mr. Tomitake isn't doing so badly himself'; New: 'you're not exactly standing still yourself, Tomitake-ojisama'. New preserves the Japanese honorific おじさま and changes from third person to direct address, matching the Japanese.

TextENGNew: "Wh — no, I didn't mean anything by it...!"

"Yeah, yeah.
Work hard and get us some great photos～!
Bye-byeee!!"
Mion was clearly itching to shoo this smooth-talking old man away as fast as possible.
Judging by the way she spoke, though... it was clear Mion knew Tomitake-san fairly well.
"How about this year, ojisama? Staying through the Watanagashi?"

"Yeah, that's the plan.
Once I've gotten my shots of the festival, I'll be heading back to Tokyo."

"Being a photographer really is a cushy life, isn't it!
Hurry up and win some big prize and make a name for yourself!
You're sacrificing your prime marrying years for this camera of yours, after all!"
changeReason: Old: 'You spent all your best years on photography'; New: 'You're sacrificing your prime marrying years for this camera of yours'. Japanese 婚期逃して specifically means 'missing your window for marriage', not generic 'best years'.

TextENGNew:
"I-I don't think that's quite right!
They say a man only starts to develop his character once he hits thirty, don't they...?"
"Who wants flavor coming off a man? That's just gross!"

"Ha, ah, ahahahaha!
Oh dear, I'd better get back to the inn before it gets dark!"
Tomitake-san flashed a rueful smile and began to slip away.
He was no match for Mion at all, it seemed.
"Well then, you two. See you at the festival."
Giving a bright wave, Tomitake-san vanished into the chorus of higurashi.

"I wonder if someone like him can actually make it big.
He promised he'd show one of my photos if he ever opened a solo exhibition, but... that day seems pretty far off."
"So you actually know Tomitake-san."
"Well, acquaintance is maybe too strong a word — but you know how it is around here. Outsiders stand out immediately."
"The way you two talk doesn't sound like you just met yesterday."

"Tomitake-san comes to Hinamizawa fairly often, actually.
Maybe two or three times a year?
He says he's out here photographing seasonal landscapes and wild birds. Though I doubt the photos are anything special!"
The Tomitake-san Mion described was quite a colorful character.
The Tomitake-san in my head, though... was somewhat more mysterious.
The thought slipped out before I could stop it.
"...You really think Tomitake-san comes here just to photograph birds?"
Mion blinked at me, tilting her head with a "why do you ask?"

"It just feels like... maybe he has some other reason for coming here, apart from photography.
Don't you get that sense from him?"
Something like... that incident... the foreman's murder...
changeReason: Old: 'that incident... the dismemberment...'; New: 'that incident... the foreman's murder...'. Japanese 現場監督の殺人事件 means 'foreman's murder case', not 'dismemberment'. Different crime description.

TextENGNew: Running into the man everywhere I go — it was hard not to read something into it.

"Oh? You think so too?
...If that's the case, you've got pretty sharp instincts, Kei-cha～n!"
She was agreeing with me — and yet her tone was thoroughly playful.
"You know something, Mion?"

"Oh, perhaps～!
The others might not have caught on, but... this old lady's eyes don't miss a thing～... ☆"
changeReason: Old: 'He might be able to pull one over on everyone else... but he can't fool this old man'; New: 'The others might not have caught on, but... this old lady's eyes don't miss a thing'. Old says 'old man' but Mion refers to herself as おじさん (a masculine self-reference she uses as a joke). The new uses 'old lady' which while different from the literal is also not quite right. However both miss the joke.

TextENGNew: Mion was being pointedly cagey about it — but it wasn't hard to tell that whatever she was thinking was entirely different from my own suspicions.
With those one-sided worries about Tomitake-san suddenly dissolving, I felt my shoulders relax.
Only then did I remember how clear the evening air of Hinamizawa truly was.
"...Aaaaaaaaah～!!"

I emptied my lungs completely, then breathed in just as deep.
It smelled of twilight.
"What's gotten into you, Kei-chan?"

"The higurashi...
I never realized their song could feel this good."
"Ahahahaha! A bit late to be discovering that, Kei-chan."
Neither of us was quite sure who started it. We just laughed, the two of us together.

"I wonder what Rena's up to right now.
I hope she got to lavish enough affection on Rika-chan."
"She's probably right in the middle of inviting her over with promises of a home-cooked dinner."
"Hope she manages to reel them in."

"Hard to say.
Rika-chan is surprisingly good at wriggling out of things, though!"

"No kidding — totally agree.
That head-patting penalty game was definitely Rika-chan's idea."

"Same here～!
The only person who could get away with patting the principal's head is Rika-chan, after all."
Aimless chatter.
The evening air and the song of the higurashi gently drew the heat from the burning day, cooling it from the inside out.
